summary: Re-verified the resume-download bug fix after switching to a SAME-ORIGIN SYNCHRONOUS <a download> click. All 4 entry points (resume-button, hero-download-resume, contact-resume, command-resume) trigger a real Playwright download event with suggested_filename='Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf' served from the SAME-ORIGIN URL https://zeel-craft.preview.emergentagent.com/Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf. Page URL is unchanged after every click, no new tab is opened, window.open is never called, and no 'Resume coming soon' toast appears.
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx::downloadResume() is now synchronous: builds <a href=profile.resumeUrl download='Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf'>, clicks, removes. No fetch/await/target=_blank. This is the correct Safari/strict-browser-safe pattern and matches the reported fix exactly.
Dead 'Resume coming soon' toast branch is unreachable because profile.resumeUrl is always populated ('/Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf'). Minor cleanup opportunity, not a bug.
PDF is served same-origin: HTTP/2 200, content-type application/pdf, content-length 163097, with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * — fully compliant for download.
test_credentials: None — frontend-only static site, no auth.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified. For each of the 4 triggers (data-testids: resume-button, hero-download-resume, contact-resume, command-resume) Playwright's page.expect_download fired with suggested_filename='Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf' and download.url='https://zeel-craft.preview.emergentagent.com/Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf' (same-origin). page.url was unchanged before vs after every click ('https://zeel-craft.preview.emergentagent.com/'), context.pages count stayed at 1 (no new tab opened), and an init-script monkeypatch of window.open recorded ZERO calls. No 'coming soon' toast detected. Only console errors observed are 3rd-party (Bing Maps invalid credentials, Geolocation permissions, Dice game React 'class' warning) — all unrelated to the resume download flow. The command palette was opened via the 'open-command-palette' button before clicking 'command-resume'. PDF endpoint validated separately via curl -I: HTTP/2 200, content-type: application/pdf, content-length: 163097.
rca of the issue: Previous iteration (3) used an async blob fetch which lost the user-gesture context in strict browsers (Safari), causing the click handler's fallback to navigate to the cross-origin PDF URL. Current fix moves the PDF to public/Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf (same-origin) and uses a fully synchronous <a href='/Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf' download='Zeel_Patel_Resume.pdf'> programmatic click within the user-gesture frame — no fetch, no await, no target='_blank'. Verified via Playwright download event + page.url before/after equality + context.pages count + window.open monkeypatch across all 4 triggers.
